NKJV.2: Then He said to them, “The harvest truly is great, but the laborers are few; therefore pray the Lord of the harvest to send out laborers into His harvest.
NKJV.3: Go your way;  behold, I send you out as lambs among wolves.
NKJV.4: Carry neither money bag, knapsack, nor sandals; and  greet no one along the road.
NKJV.5: But whatever house you enter, first say, ‘Peace to this house.’
NKJV.6: And if a son of peace is there, your peace will rest on it; if not, it will return to you.
NKJV.7: And remain in the same house,  eating and drinking such things as they give, for  the laborer is worthy of his wages. Do not go from house to house.
NKJV.8: Whatever city you enter, and they receive you, eat such things as are set before you.
NKJV.9: And heal the sick there, and say to them,  ‘The kingdom of God has come near to you.’
NKJV.10: But whatever city you enter, and they do not receive you, go out into its streets and say,
NKJV.11: ‘The very dust of your city which clings to us we wipe off against you. Nevertheless know this, that the kingdom of God has come near you.’
NKJV.12: But I say to you that  it will be more tolerable in that Day for Sodom than for that city.
NKJV.13: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida!  For if the mighty works which were done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
NKJV.14: But it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the judgment than for you.
NKJV.15: And you, Capernaum, who are  exalted to heaven,  will be brought down to Hades.
NKJV.16: He who hears you hears Me,  he who rejects you rejects Me, and  he who rejects Me rejects Him who sent Me.”
NKJV.17: Then the seventy returned with joy, saying, “Lord, even the demons are subject to us in Your name.”
NKJV.18: And He said to them, “I saw Satan fall like lightning from heaven.
NKJV.19: Behold,  I give you the authority to trample on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy, and nothing shall by any means hurt you.
NKJV.20: Nevertheless do not rejoice in this, that the spirits are subject to you, but rather rejoice because  your names are written in heaven.”
NKJV.21: In that hour Jesus rejoiced in the Spirit and said, “I thank You, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that You have hidden these things from the wise and prudent and revealed them to babes. Even so, Father, for so it seemed good in Your sight.

NKJV.24: for I tell you that many prophets and kings have desired to see what you see, and have not seen it, and to hear what you hear, and have not heard it.”

NKJV.33: But a certain  Samaritan, as he journeyed, came where he was. And when he saw him, he had  compassion.

NKJV.36: So which of these three do you think was neighbor to him who fell among the thieves?”
NKJV.37: And he said, “He who showed mercy on him.” Then Jesus said to him, “Go and do likewise.”

NKJV.39: And she had a sister called Mary, who also sat at Jesus’ feet and heard His word.
NKJV.40: But Martha was distracted with much serving, and she approached Him and said, “Lord, do You not care that my sister has left me to serve alone? Therefore tell her to help me.”
NKJV.41: And Jesus answered and said to her, “Martha, Martha, you are worried and troubled about many things.
NKJV.42: But  one thing is needed, and Mary has chosen that good part, which will not be taken away from her.”

ESV.1: After this the Lord appointed seventy-two  others and sent them on ahead of him, two by two, into every town and place where he himself was about to go.
ESV.2: And he said to them, “The harvest is plentiful, but the laborers are few. Therefore pray earnestly to the Lord of the harvest to send out laborers into his harvest.
ESV.3: Go your way; behold, I am sending you out as lambs in the midst of wolves.
ESV.4: Carry no moneybag, no knapsack, no sandals, and greet no one on the road.
ESV.5: Whatever house you enter, first say, ‘Peace be to this house!’
ESV.6: And if a son of peace is there, your peace will rest upon him. But if not, it will return to you.
ESV.7: And remain in the same house, eating and drinking what they provide, for the laborer deserves his wages. Do not go from house to house.
ESV.8: Whenever you enter a town and they receive you, eat what is set before you.
ESV.9: Heal the sick in it and say to them, ‘The kingdom of God has come near to you.’
ESV.10: But whenever you enter a town and they do not receive you, go into its streets and say,
ESV.11: ‘Even the dust of your town that clings to our feet we wipe off against you. Nevertheless know this, that the kingdom of God has come near.’
ESV.12: I tell you, it will be more bearable on that day for Sodom than for that town.
ESV.13: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the mighty works done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
ESV.14: But it will be more bearable in the judgment for Tyre and Sidon than for you.
ESV.15: And you, Capernaum, will you be exalted to heaven? You shall be brought down to Hades.
ESV.16: “The one who hears you hears me, and the one who rejects you rejects me, and the one who rejects me rejects him who sent me.”
ESV.17: The seventy-two returned with joy, saying, “Lord, even the demons are subject to us in your name!”
ESV.18: And he said to them,  “I saw Satan fall like lightning from heaven.
ESV.19: Behold, I have given you authority to tread on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy, and nothing shall hurt you.
ESV.20: Nevertheless, do not rejoice in this, that the spirits are subject to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.”
ESV.21: In that same hour he rejoiced in the Holy Spirit and said, “I thank you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that you have hidden these things from the wise and understanding and revealed them to little children; yes, Father, for such was your gracious will.
ESV.22: All things have been handed over to me by my Father, and no one knows who the Son is except the Father, or who the Father is except the Son and anyone to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.”
ESV.23: Then turning to the disciples he said privately,  “Blessed are the eyes that see what you see!
ESV.24: For I tell you that many prophets and kings desired to see what you see, and did not see it, and to hear what you hear, and did not hear it.”
ESV.25: And behold, a lawyer stood up to put him to the test, saying, “Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?”
ESV.26: He said to him, “What is written in the Law? How do you read it?”
ESV.27: And he answered, “You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your strength and with all your mind, and your neighbor as yourself.”
ESV.28: And he said to him, “You have answered correctly; do this, and you will live.”
ESV.29: But he, desiring to justify himself, said to Jesus, “And who is my neighbor?”
ESV.30: Jesus replied, “A man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and he fell among robbers, who stripped him and beat him and departed, leaving him half dead.
ESV.31: Now by chance a priest was going down that road, and when he saw him he passed by on the other side.
ESV.32: So likewise a Levite, when he came to the place and saw him, passed by on the other side.
ESV.33: But a Samaritan, as he journeyed, came to where he was, and when he saw him, he had compassion.
ESV.34: He went to him and bound up his wounds, pouring on oil and wine. Then he set him on his own animal and brought him to an inn and took care of him.
ESV.35: And the next day he took out two denarii  and gave them to the innkeeper, saying, ‘Take care of him, and whatever more you spend, I will repay you when I come back.’
ESV.36: Which of these three, do you think, proved to be a neighbor to the man who fell among the robbers?”
ESV.37: He said, “The one who showed him mercy.” And Jesus said to him, “You go, and do likewise.”
ESV.38: Now as they went on their way, Jesus  entered a village. And a woman named Martha welcomed him into her house.
ESV.39: And she had a sister called Mary, who sat at the Lord's feet and listened to his teaching.
ESV.40: But Martha was distracted with much serving. And she went up to him and said, “Lord, do you not care that my sister has left me to serve alone? Tell her then to help me.”
ESV.41: But the Lord answered her, “Martha, Martha, you are anxious and troubled about many things,
ESV.42: but one thing is necessary.  Mary has chosen the good portion, which will not be taken away from her.”

NASB.1: Now after this the Lord appointed seventy others, and sent them in pairs ahead of Him to every city and place where He Himself was going to come.
NASB.2: And He was saying to them, 'The harvest is plentiful, but the laborers are few; therefore beseech the Lord of the harvest to send out laborers into His harvest.
NASB.3: 'Go; behold, I send you out as lambs in the midst of wolves.
NASB.4: 'Carry no money belt, no bag, no shoes; and greet no one on the way.
NASB.5: 'Whatever house you enter, first say, 'Peace [be] to this house.'
NASB.6: 'If a man of peace is there, your peace will rest on him; but if not, it will return to you.
NASB.7: 'Stay in that house, eating and drinking what they give you; for the laborer is worthy of his wages. Do not keep moving from house to house.
NASB.8: 'Whatever city you enter and they receive you, eat what is set before you;
NASB.9: and heal those in it who are sick, and say to them, 'The kingdom of God has come near to you.'
NASB.10: 'But whatever city you enter and they do not receive you, go out into its streets and say,
NASB.11: 'Even the dust of your city which clings to our feet we wipe off [in protest] against you; yet be sure of this, that the kingdom of God has come near.'
NASB.12: 'I say to you, it will be more tolerable in that day for Sodom than for that city.
NASB.13: 'Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles had been performed in Tyre and Sidon which occurred in you, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
NASB.14: 'But it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in the judgment than for you.
NASB.15: 'And you, Capernaum, will not be exalted to heaven, will you? You will be brought down to Hades!
NASB.16: 'The one who listens to you listens to Me, and the one who rejects you rejects Me; and he who rejects Me rejects the One who sent Me.'
NASB.17: The seventy returned with joy, saying, 'Lord, even the demons are subject to us in Your name.'
NASB.18: And He said to them, 'I was watching Satan fall from heaven like lightning.
NASB.19: 'Behold, I have given you authority to tread on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy, and nothing will injure you.
NASB.20: 'Nevertheless do not rejoice in this, that the spirits are subject to you, but rejoice that your names are recorded in heaven.'
NASB.21: At that very time He rejoiced greatly in the Holy Spirit, and said, 'I praise You, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that You have hidden these things from [the] wise and intelligent and have revealed them to infants. Yes, Father, for this way was well-pleasing in Your sight.
NASB.22: 'All things have been handed over to Me by My Father, and no one knows who the Son is except the Father, and who the Father is except the Son, and anyone to whom the Son wills to reveal [Him].'
NASB.23: Turning to the disciples, He said privately, 'Blessed [are] the eyes which see the things you see,
NASB.24: for I say to you, that many prophets and kings wished to see the things which you see, and did not see [them], and to hear the things which you hear, and did not hear [them].'
NASB.25: And a lawyer stood up and put Him to the test, saying, 'Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?'
NASB.26: And He said to him, 'What is written in the Law? How does it read to you?'
NASB.27: And he answered, 'YOU SHALL LOVE THE LORD YOUR GOD WITH ALL YOUR HEART, AND WITH ALL YOUR SOUL, AND WITH ALL YOUR STRENGTH, AND WITH ALL YOUR MIND; AND YOUR NEIGHBOR AS YOURSELF.'
NASB.28: And He said to him, 'You have answered correctly; DO THIS AND YOU WILL LIVE.'
NASB.29: But wishing to justify himself, he said to Jesus, 'And who is my neighbor?'
NASB.30: Jesus replied and said, 'A man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and fell among robbers, and they stripped him and beat him, and went away leaving him half dead.
NASB.31: 'And by chance a priest was going down on that road, and when he saw him, he passed by on the other side.
NASB.32: 'Likewise a Levite also, when he came to the place and saw him, passed by on the other side.
NASB.33: 'But a Samaritan, who was on a journey, came upon him; and when he saw him, he felt compassion,
NASB.34: and came to him and bandaged up his wounds, pouring oil and wine on [them]; and he put him on his own beast, and brought him to an inn and took care of him.
NASB.35: 'On the next day he took out two denarii and gave them to the innkeeper and said, 'Take care of him; and whatever more you spend, when I return I will repay you.'
NASB.36: 'Which of these three do you think proved to be a neighbor to the man who fell into the robbers' [hands]?'
NASB.37: And he said, 'The one who showed mercy toward him.' Then Jesus said to him, 'Go and do the same.'
NASB.38: Now as they were traveling along, He entered a village; and a woman named Martha welcomed Him into her home.
NASB.39: She had a sister called Mary, who was seated at the Lord's feet, listening to His word.
NASB.40: But Martha was distracted with all her preparations; and she came up [to Him] and said, 'Lord, do You not care that my sister has left me to do all the serving alone? Then tell her to help me.'
NASB.41: But the Lord answered and said to her, 'Martha, Martha, you are worried and bothered about so many things;
NASB.42: but [only] one thing is necessary, for Mary has chosen the good part, which shall not be taken away from her.'

NLT.1: The Lord now chose seventy-two other disciples and sent them ahead in pairs to all the towns and places he planned to visit.
NLT.2: These were his instructions to them: “The harvest is great, but the workers are few. So pray to the Lord who is in charge of the harvest; ask him to send more workers into his fields.
NLT.3: Now go, and remember that I am sending you out as lambs among wolves.
NLT.4: Don’t take any money with you, nor a traveler’s bag, nor an extra pair of sandals. And don’t stop to greet anyone on the road.
NLT.5: “Whenever you enter someone’s home, first say, ‘May God’s peace be on this house.’
NLT.6: If those who live there are peaceful, the blessing will stand; if they are not, the blessing will return to you.
NLT.7: Don’t move around from home to home. Stay in one place, eating and drinking what they provide. Don’t hesitate to accept hospitality, because those who work deserve their pay.
NLT.8: “If you enter a town and it welcomes you, eat whatever is set before you.
NLT.9: Heal the sick, and tell them, ‘The Kingdom of God is near you now.’
NLT.10: But if a town refuses to welcome you, go out into its streets and say,
NLT.11: ‘We wipe even the dust of your town from our feet to show that we have abandoned you to your fate. And know this — the Kingdom of God is near!’
NLT.12: I assure you, even wicked Sodom will be better off than such a town on judgment day.
NLT.13: “What sorrow awaits you, Korazin and Bethsaida! For if the miracles I did in you had been done in wicked Tyre and Sidon, their people would have repented of their sins long ago, clothing themselves in burlap and throwing ashes on their heads to show their remorse.
NLT.14: Yes, Tyre and Sidon will be better off on judgment day than you.
NLT.15: And you people of Capernaum, will you be honored in heaven? No, you will go down to the place of the dead. ”
NLT.16: Then he said to the disciples, “Anyone who accepts your message is also accepting me. And anyone who rejects you is rejecting me. And anyone who rejects me is rejecting God, who sent me.”
NLT.17: When the seventy-two disciples returned, they joyfully reported to him, “Lord, even the demons obey us when we use your name!”
NLT.18: “Yes,” he told them, “I saw Satan fall from heaven like lightning!
NLT.19: Look, I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy, and you can walk among snakes and scorpions and crush them. Nothing will injure you.
NLT.20: But don’t rejoice because evil spirits obey you; rejoice because your names are registered in heaven.”
NLT.21: At that same time Jesus was filled with the joy of the Holy Spirit, and he said, “O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, thank you for hiding these things from those who think themselves wise and clever, and for revealing them to the childlike. Yes, Father, it pleased you to do it this way.
NLT.22: “My Father has entrusted everything to me. No one truly knows the Son except the Father, and no one truly knows the Father except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.”
NLT.23: Then when they were alone, he turned to the disciples and said, “Blessed are the eyes that see what you have seen.
NLT.24: I tell you, many prophets and kings longed to see what you see, but they didn’t see it. And they longed to hear what you hear, but they didn’t hear it.”
NLT.25: One day an expert in religious law stood up to test Jesus by asking him this question: “Teacher, what should I do to inherit eternal life?”
NLT.26: Jesus replied, “What does the law of Moses say? How do you read it?”
NLT.27: The man answered, “‘You must love the LORD your God with all your heart, all your soul, all your strength, and all your mind.’ And, ‘Love your neighbor as yourself.’”
NLT.28: “Right!” Jesus told him. “Do this and you will live!”
NLT.29: The man wanted to justify his actions, so he asked Jesus, “And who is my neighbor?”
NLT.30: Jesus replied with a story: “A Jewish man was traveling from Jerusalem down to Jericho, and he was attacked by bandits. They stripped him of his clothes, beat him up, and left him half dead beside the road.
NLT.31: “By chance a priest came along. But when he saw the man lying there, he crossed to the other side of the road and passed him by.
NLT.32: A Temple assistant walked over and looked at him lying there, but he also passed by on the other side.
NLT.33: “Then a despised Samaritan came along, and when he saw the man, he felt compassion for him.
NLT.34: Going over to him, the Samaritan soothed his wounds with olive oil and wine and bandaged them. Then he put the man on his own donkey and took him to an inn, where he took care of him.
NLT.35: The next day he handed the innkeeper two silver coins, telling him, ‘Take care of this man. If his bill runs higher than this, I’ll pay you the next time I’m here.’
NLT.36: “Now which of these three would you say was a neighbor to the man who was attacked by bandits?” Jesus asked.
NLT.37: The man replied, “The one who showed him mercy.” Then Jesus said, “Yes, now go and do the same.”
NLT.38: As Jesus and the disciples continued on their way to Jerusalem, they came to a certain village where a woman named Martha welcomed him into her home.
NLT.39: Her sister, Mary, sat at the Lord’s feet, listening to what he taught.
NLT.40: But Martha was distracted by the big dinner she was preparing. She came to Jesus and said, “Lord, doesn’t it seem unfair to you that my sister just sits here while I do all the work? Tell her to come and help me.”
NLT.41: But the Lord said to her, “My dear Martha, you are worried and upset over all these details!
NLT.42: There is only one thing worth being concerned about. Mary has discovered it, and it will not be taken away from her.”

WEB.1: Now after these things, the Lord also appointed seventy others, and sent them two by two ahead of him  into every city and place, where he was about to come.
WEB.2: Then he said to them, “The harvest is indeed plentiful, but the laborers are few. Pray therefore to the Lord of the harvest, that he may send out laborers into his harvest.
WEB.3: Go your ways. Behold, I send you out as lambs among wolves.
WEB.4: Carry no purse, nor wallet, nor sandals. Greet no one on the way.
WEB.5: Into whatever house you enter, first say, ‘Peace be to this house.’
WEB.6: If a son of peace is there, your peace will rest on him; but if not, it will return to you.
WEB.7: Remain in that same house, eating and drinking the things they give, for the laborer is worthy of his wages. Don’t go from house to house.
WEB.8: Into whatever city you enter, and they receive you, eat the things that are set before you.
WEB.9: Heal the sick who are therein, and tell them, ‘God’s Kingdom has come near to you.’
WEB.10: But into whatever city you enter, and they don’t receive you, go out into its streets and say,
WEB.11: ‘Even the dust from your city that clings to us, we wipe off against you. Nevertheless know this, that God’s Kingdom has come near to you.’
WEB.12: I tell you, it will be more tolerable in that day for Sodom than for that city.
WEB.13: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidon which were done in you, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
WEB.14: But it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in the judgment than for you.
WEB.15: You, Capernaum, who are exalted to heaven, will be brought down to Hades.
WEB.16: Whoever listens to you listens to me, and whoever rejects you rejects me. Whoever rejects me rejects him who sent me.”
WEB.17: The seventy returned with joy, saying, “Lord, even the demons are subject to us in your name!”
WEB.18: He said to them, “I saw Satan having fallen like lightning from heaven.
WEB.19: Behold, I give you authority to tread on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will in any way hurt you.
WEB.20: Nevertheless, don’t rejoice in this, that the spirits are subject to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.”
WEB.21: In that same hour Jesus rejoiced in the Holy Spirit, and said, “I thank you, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that you have hidden these things from the wise and understanding, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for so it was well-pleasing in your sight.”
WEB.22: Turning to the disciples, he said, “All things have been delivered to me by my Father. No one knows who the Son is, except the Father, and who the Father is, except the Son, and he to whomever the Son desires to reveal him.”
WEB.23: Turning to the disciples, he said privately, “Blessed are the eyes which see the things that you see,
WEB.24: for I tell you that many prophets and kings desired to see the things which you see, and didn’t see them, and to hear the things which you hear, and didn’t hear them.”
WEB.25: Behold, a certain lawyer stood up and tested him, saying, “Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?”
WEB.26: He said to him, “What is written in the law? How do you read it?”
WEB.27: He answered, “You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your strength, and with all your mind;  and your neighbor as yourself.”
WEB.28: He said to him, “You have answered correctly. Do this, and you will live.”
WEB.29: But he, desiring to justify himself, asked Jesus, “Who is my neighbor?”
WEB.30: Jesus answered, “A certain man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and he fell among robbers, who both stripped him and beat him, and departed, leaving him half dead.
WEB.31: By chance a certain priest was going down that way. When he saw him, he passed by on the other side.
WEB.32: In the same way a Levite also, when he came to the place, and saw him, passed by on the other side.
WEB.33: But a certain Samaritan, as he traveled, came where he was. When he saw him, he was moved with compassion,
WEB.34: came to him, and bound up his wounds, pouring on oil and wine. He set him on his own animal, and brought him to an inn, and took care of him.
WEB.35: On the next day, when he departed, he took out two denarii, and gave them to the host, and said to him, ‘Take care of him. Whatever you spend beyond that, I will repay you when I return.’
WEB.36: Now which of these three do you think seemed to be a neighbor to him who fell among the robbers?”
WEB.37: He said, “He who showed mercy on him.” Then Jesus said to him, “Go and do likewise.”
WEB.38: As they went on their way, he entered into a certain village, and a certain woman named Martha received him into her house.
WEB.39: She had a sister called Mary, who also sat at Jesus’ feet, and heard his word.
WEB.40: But Martha was distracted with much serving, and she came up to him, and said, “Lord, don’t you care that my sister left me to serve alone? Ask her therefore to help me.”
WEB.41: Jesus answered her, “Martha, Martha, you are anxious and troubled about many things,
WEB.42: but one thing is needed. Mary has chosen the good part, which will not be taken away from her.”

AMP.1: Now after this the Lord appointed seventy others, and sent them out ahead of Him, two by two, into every city and place where He was about to go.
AMP.2: He was saying to them, "The harvest is abundant [for there are many who need to hear the good news about salvation], but the workers [those available to proclaim the message of salvation] are few. Therefore, [prayerfully] ask the Lord of the harvest to send out workers into His harvest.
AMP.3: Go your way; listen carefully: I am sending you out like lambs among wolves.
AMP.4: Do not carry a money belt, a provision bag, or [extra] sandals; and do not greet anyone along the way [who would delay you].
AMP.5: Whatever house you enter, first say, ' Peace [that is, a blessing of well-being and prosperity, the favor of God] to this house.'
AMP.6: And if anyone of peace is there [someone who is sweet-spirited and hospitable], your [blessing of] peace will rest on him; but if not, it will return to you.
AMP.7: Stay in that house, eating and drinking what they provide, for the laborer is worthy of his wages. Do not move from house to house.
AMP.8: Whenever you go into a city and they welcome you, eat what is set before you;
AMP.9: and heal those in it who are sick [authenticating your message], and say to them, 'The kingdom of God has come near to you.'
AMP.10: But whatever city you enter and they do not welcome you, go out into its streets and say,
AMP.11: 'Even the dust of your city which clings to our feet we wipe off in protest against you [breaking all ties]; yet understand this, that the kingdom of God has come near [and you rejected it].'
AMP.12: I tell you, it will be more bearable in that day [of judgment] for Sodom than for that city.
AMP.13: "Woe (judgment is coming) to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles performed in you had been performed in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented and changed their minds long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes [to show deep regret for sin].
AMP.14: However, it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in the judgment than for you.
AMP.15: And you, Capernaum, will not be exalted to heaven, will you? You will descend to Hades (the realm of the dead).
AMP.16: "The one who listens to you listens to Me; and the one who rejects you rejects Me; and the one who rejects Me rejects Him [My heavenly Father] who sent Me."
AMP.17: The seventy returned with joy, saying, "Lord, even the demons are subject to us in Your name."
AMP.18: He said to them, "I watched Satan fall from heaven like [a flash of] lightning.
AMP.19: Listen carefully: I have given you authority [that you now possess] to tread on serpents and scorpions, and [the ability to exercise authority] over all the power of the enemy (Satan); and nothing will [in any way] harm you.
AMP.20: Nevertheless do not rejoice at this, that the spirits are subject to you, but rejoice that your names are recorded in heaven."
AMP.21: In that very hour He was overjoyed and rejoiced greatly in the Holy Spirit, and He said, "I praise You, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that You have hidden these things [relating to salvation] from the wise and intelligent, and have revealed them to infants [the childlike and untaught]. Yes, Father, for this way was [Your gracious will and choice, and was] well-pleasing in Your sight.
AMP.22: All things have been transferred and turned over to Me by My Father and no one knows who the Son is except the Father, or who the Father is except the Son, and anyone to whom the Son wishes to reveal Him."
AMP.23: Then turning to His disciples, Jesus said privately, "Blessed [joyful, spiritually enlightened, and favored by God] are the eyes which see what you see,
AMP.24: for I say to you that many prophets and kings longed to see what you see, and they did not see it; and to hear what you hear, and did not hear it."
AMP.25: And a certain lawyer [an expert in Mosaic Law] stood up to test Him, saying, "Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?"
AMP.26: Jesus said to him, "What is written in the Law? How do you read it?"
AMP.27: And he replied, "<e>You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your strength, and with all your mind; and your neighbor as yourself</e>."
AMP.28: Jesus said to him, "You have answered correctly; do this habitually and you will live."
AMP.29: But he, wishing to justify and vindicate himself, asked Jesus, "And who is my neighbor?"
AMP.30: Jesus replied, "A man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and he encountered robbers, who stripped him of his clothes [and belongings], beat him, and went their way [unconcerned], leaving him half dead.
AMP.31: Now by coincidence a priest was going down that road, and when he saw him, he passed by on the other side.
AMP.32: Likewise a Levite also came down to the place and saw him, and passed by on the other side [of the road].
AMP.33: But a Samaritan (foreigner), who was traveling, came upon him; and when he saw him, he was deeply moved with compassion [for him],
AMP.34: and went to him and bandaged up his wounds, pouring oil and wine on them [to sooth and disinfect the injuries]; and he put him on his own pack-animal, and brought him to an inn and took care of him.
AMP.35: On the next day he took out two denarii (two days' wages) and gave them to the innkeeper, and said, 'Take care of him; and whatever more you spend, I will repay you when I return.'
AMP.36: Which of these three do you think proved himself a neighbor to the man who encountered the robbers?"
AMP.37: He answered, "The one who showed compassion and mercy to him." Then Jesus said to him, "Go and constantly do the same."
AMP.38: Now while they were on their way, Jesus entered a village [called Bethany], and a woman named Martha welcomed Him into her home.
AMP.39: She had a sister named Mary, who seated herself at the Lord's feet and was continually listening to His teaching.
AMP.40: But Martha was very busy and distracted with all of her serving responsibilities; and she approached Him and said, "Lord, is it of no concern to You that my sister has left me to do the serving alone? Tell her to help me and do her part."
AMP.41: But the Lord replied to her, "Martha, Martha, you are worried and bothered and anxious about so many things;
AMP.42: but only one thing is necessary, for Mary has chosen the good part [that which is to her advantage], which will not be taken away from her."

MSG.1: Later the Master selected seventy and sent them ahead of him in pairs to every town and place where he intended to go.
MSG.2: He gave them this charge: "What a huge harvest! And how few the harvest hands. So on your knees; ask the God of the Harvest to send harvest hands.
MSG.3: "On your way! But be careful—this is hazardous work. You're like lambs in a wolf pack.
MSG.4: "Travel light. Comb and toothbrush and no extra luggage. "Don't loiter and make small talk with everyone you meet along the way.
MSG.5: "When you enter a home, greet the family, 'Peace.'
MSG.6: If your greeting is received, then it's a good place to stay. But if it's not received, take it back and get out. Don't impose yourself.
MSG.7: "Stay at one home, taking your meals there, for a worker deserves three square meals. Don't move from house to house, looking for the best cook in town.
MSG.8: "When you enter a town and are received, eat what they set before you,
MSG.9: heal anyone who is sick, and tell them, 'God's kingdom is right on your doorstep!'
MSG.10: "When you enter a town and are not received, go out in the street and say,
MSG.11: 'The only thing we got from you is the dirt on our feet, and we're giving it back. Did you have any idea that God's kingdom was right on your doorstep?'
MSG.12: Sodom will have it better on Judgment Day than the town that rejects you.
MSG.13: "Doom, Chorazin! Doom, Bethsaida! If Tyre and Sidon had been given half the chances given you, they'd have been on their knees long ago, repenting and crying for mercy.
MSG.14: Tyre and Sidon will have it easy on Judgment Day compared to you.
MSG.15: "And you, Capernaum! Do you think you're about to be promoted to heaven? Think again. You're on a mud slide to hell.
MSG.16: "The one who listens to you, listens to me. The one who rejects you, rejects me. And rejecting me is the same as rejecting God, who sent me."
MSG.17: The seventy came back triumphant. "Master, even the demons danced to your tune!"
MSG.18: Jesus said, "I know. I saw Satan fall, a bolt of lightning out of the sky.
MSG.19: See what I've given you? Safe passage as you walk on snakes and scorpions, and protection from every assault of the Enemy. No one can put a hand on you.
MSG.20: All the same, the great triumph is not in your authority over evil, but in God's authority over you and presence with you. Not what you do for God but what God does for you—that's the agenda for rejoicing."
MSG.21: At that, Jesus rejoiced, exuberant in the Holy Spirit. "I thank you, Father, Master of heaven and earth, that you hid these things from the know-it-alls and showed them to these innocent newcomers. Yes, Father, it pleased you to do it this way.
MSG.22: "I've been given it all by my Father! Only the Father knows who the Son is and only the Son knows who the Father is. The Son can introduce the Father to anyone he wants to."
MSG.23: He then turned in a private aside to his disciples. "Fortunate the eyes that see what you're seeing!
MSG.24: There are plenty of prophets and kings who would have given their right arm to see what you are seeing but never got so much as a glimpse, to hear what you are hearing but never got so much as a whisper."
MSG.25: Just then a religion scholar stood up with a question to test Jesus. "Teacher, what do I need to do to get eternal life?"
MSG.26: He answered, "What's written in God's Law? How do you interpret it?"
MSG.27: He said, "That you love the Lord your God with all your passion and prayer and muscle and intelligence—and that you love your neighbor as well as you do yourself."
MSG.28: "Good answer!" said Jesus. "Do it and you'll live."
MSG.29: Looking for a loophole, he asked, "And just how would you define 'neighbor'?"
MSG.30: Jesus answered by telling a story. "There was once a man traveling from Jerusalem to Jericho. On the way he was attacked by robbers. They took his clothes, beat him up, and went off leaving him half-dead.
MSG.31: Luckily, a priest was on his way down the same road, but when he saw him he angled across to the other side.
MSG.32: Then a Levite religious man showed up; he also avoided the injured man.
MSG.33: "A Samaritan traveling the road came on him. When he saw the man's condition, his heart went out to him.
MSG.34: He gave him first aid, disinfecting and bandaging his wounds. Then he lifted him onto his donkey, led him to an inn, and made him comfortable.
MSG.35: In the morning he took out two silver coins and gave them to the innkeeper, saying, 'Take good care of him. If it costs any more, put it on my bill—I'll pay you on my way back.'
MSG.36: "What do you think? Which of the three became a neighbor to the man attacked by robbers?"
MSG.37: "The one who treated him kindly," the religion scholar responded. Jesus said, "Go and do the same."
MSG.38: As they continued their travel, Jesus entered a village. A woman by the name of Martha welcomed him and made him feel quite at home.
MSG.39: She had a sister, Mary, who sat before the Master, hanging on every word he said.
MSG.40: But Martha was pulled away by all she had to do in the kitchen. Later, she stepped in, interrupting them. "Master, don't you care that my sister has abandoned the kitchen to me? Tell her to lend me a hand."
MSG.41: The Master said, "Martha, dear Martha, you're fussing far too much and getting yourself worked up over nothing.
MSG.42: One thing only is essential, and Mary has chosen it—it's the main course, and won't be taken from her."

NIV.1:
Jesus Sends Out the Seventy-two
After this the Lord appointed seventy-two others and sent them two by two ahead of him to every town and place where he was about to go.
NIV.2: He told them, “The harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few. Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into his harvest field.
NIV.3: Go! I am sending you out like lambs among wolves.
NIV.4: Do not take a purse or bag or sandals; and do not greet anyone on the road.
NIV.5: “When you enter a house, first say, ‘Peace to this house.’
NIV.6: If a man of peace is there, your peace will rest on him; if not, it will return to you.
NIV.7: Stay in that house, eating and drinking whatever they give you, for the worker deserves his wages. Do not move around from house to house.
NIV.8: “When you enter a town and are welcomed, eat what is set before you.
NIV.9: Heal the sick who are there and tell them, ‘The kingdom of God is near you.’
NIV.10: But when you enter a town and are not welcomed, go into its streets and say,
NIV.11: ‘Even the dust of your town that sticks to our feet we wipe off against you. Yet be sure of this: The kingdom of God is near.’
NIV.12: I tell you, it will be more bearable on that day for Sodom than for that town.
NIV.13: “Woe to you, Korazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
NIV.14: But it will be more bearable for Tyre and Sidon at the judgment than for you.
NIV.15: And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted up to the skies? No, you will go down to the depths.
NIV.16: “He who listens to you listens to me; he who rejects you rejects me; but he who rejects me rejects him who sent me.”
NIV.17: The seventy-two returned with joy and said, “Lord, even the demons submit to us in your name.”
NIV.18: He replied, “I saw Satan fall like lightning from heaven.
NIV.19: I have given you authority to trample on snakes and scorpions and to overcome all the power of the enemy; nothing will harm you.
NIV.20: However, do not rejoice that the spirits submit to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.”
NIV.21: At that time Jesus, full of joy through the Holy Spirit, said, “I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for this was your good pleasure.
NIV.22: “All things have been committed to me by my Father. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, and no one knows who the Father is except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.”
NIV.23: Then he turned to his disciples and said privately, “Blessed are the eyes that see what you see.
NIV.24: For I tell you that many prophets and kings wanted to see what you see but did not see it, and to hear what you hear but did not hear it.”
NIV.25:
The Parable of the Good Samaritan
On one occasion an expert in the law stood up to test Jesus. “Teacher,” he asked, “what must I do to inherit eternal life?”
NIV.26: “What is written in the Law?” he replied. “How do you read it?”
NIV.27: He answered: “‘Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your strength and with all your mind’; and, ‘Love your neighbor as yourself.’”
NIV.28: “You have answered correctly,” Jesus replied. “Do this and you will live.”
NIV.29: But he wanted to justify himself, so he asked Jesus, “And who is my neighbor?”
NIV.30: In reply Jesus said: “A man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, when he fell into the hands of robbers. They stripped him of his clothes, beat him and went away, leaving him half dead.
NIV.31: A priest happened to be going down the same road, and when he saw the man, he passed by on the other side.
NIV.32: So too, a Levite, when he came to the place and saw him, passed by on the other side.
NIV.33: But a Samaritan, as he traveled, came where the man was; and when he saw him, he took pity on him.
NIV.34: He went to him and bandaged his wounds, pouring on oil and wine. Then he put the man on his own donkey, took him to an inn and took care of him.
NIV.35: The next day he took out two silver coins and gave them to the innkeeper. ‘Look after him,’ he said, ‘and when I return, I will reimburse you for any extra expense you may have.’
NIV.36: “Which of these three do you think was a neighbor to the man who fell into the hands of robbers?”
NIV.37:
The expert in the law replied, “The one who had mercy on him.”
Jesus told him, “Go and do likewise.”
NIV.38:
At the Home of Martha and Mary
As Jesus and his disciples were on their way, he came to a village where a woman named Martha opened her home to him.
NIV.39: She had a sister called Mary, who sat at the Lord’s feet listening to what he said.
NIV.40: But Martha was distracted by all the preparations that had to be made. She came to him and asked, “Lord, don’t you care that my sister has left me to do the work by myself? Tell her to help me!”
NIV.41: “Martha, Martha,” the Lord answered, “you are worried and upset about many things,
NIV.42: but only one thing is needed. Mary has chosen what is better, and it will not be taken away from her.”

NET.14: But it will be more bearable for Tyre and Sidon in the judgment than for you!
NET.15: And you, Capernaum, will you be exalted to heaven? No, you will be thrown down to Hades!
NET.16: “The one who listens to you listens to me, and the one who rejects you rejects me, and the one who rejects me rejects the one who sent me.”
NET.17: Then the seventy-two returned with joy, saying, “Lord, even the demons submit to us in your name!”
NET.18: So he said to them, “I saw Satan fall like lightning from heaven.
NET.19: Look, I have given you authority to tread on snakes and scorpions and on the full force of the enemy, and nothing will hurt you.
NET.20: Nevertheless, do not rejoice that the spirits submit to you, but rejoice that your names stand written in heaven.”
NET.21: On that same occasion Jesus rejoiced in the Holy Spirit and said, “I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and intelligent, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for this was your gracious will.
NET.22: All things have been given to me by my Father. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, or who the Father is except the Son and anyone to whom the Son decides to reveal him.”
NET.23: Then Jesus turned to his disciples and said privately, “Blessed are the eyes that see what you see!
NET.24: For I tell you that many prophets and kings longed to see what you see but did not see it, and to hear what you hear but did not hear it.”
NET.25: Now an expert in religious law stood up to test Jesus, saying, “Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?”
NET.26: He said to him, “What is written in the law? How do you understand it?”
NET.27: The expert answered, “Love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your strength, and with all your mind, and love your neighbor as yourself.”
NET.28: Jesus said to him, “You have answered correctly; do this, and you will live.”
NET.29: But the expert, wanting to justify himself, said to Jesus, “And who is my neighbor?”
NET.30: Jesus replied, “A man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and fell into the hands of robbers, who stripped him, beat him up, and went off, leaving him half dead.
NET.31: Now by chance a priest was going down that road, but when he saw the injured man he passed by on the other side.
NET.32: So too a Levite, when he came up to the place and saw him, passed by on the other side.
NET.33: But a Samaritan who was traveling came to where the injured man was, and when he saw him, he felt compassion for him.
NET.34: He went up to him and bandaged his wounds, pouring oil and wine on them. Then he put him on his own animal, brought him to an inn, and took care of him.
NET.35: The next day he took out two silver coins and gave them to the innkeeper, saying, ‘Take care of him, and whatever else you spend, I will repay you when I come back this way.’
NET.36: Which of these three do you think became a neighbor to the man who fell into the hands of the robbers?”
NET.37: The expert in religious law said, “The one who showed mercy to him.” So Jesus said to him, “Go and do the same.”
NET.38: Now as they went on their way, Jesus entered a certain village where a woman named Martha welcomed him as a guest.
NET.39: She had a sister named Mary, who sat at the Lord’s feet and listened to what he said.
NET.40: But Martha was distracted with all the preparations she had to make, so she came up to him and said, “Lord, don’t you care that my sister has left me to do all the work alone? Tell her to help me.”
NET.41: But the Lord answered her, “Martha, Martha, you are worried and troubled about many things,
NET.42: but one thing is needed. Mary has chosen the best part; it will not be taken away from her.”
